rationale: Both candidates independently grep-discover the exact latent gap (hunyuan_image3_transformer.py:2238 still calling the removed get_cache_scale, matching the later #4891 issue) and both ground their findings in real file/line evidence. X covers more of the ground-truth reviewer's specific points (direct-vs-delegated correctness, the qwen2_old getattr-guard cleanup nuance, per-file validation) and its REQUEST CHANGES verdict better matches the real-world outcome that this omission later caused a bug. Y's investigation is impressively rigorous (traces actual upstream vLLM source with line citations) and raises a legitimate test-design critique, but its top-line 'APPROVE, no blockers' verdict is internally inconsistent with its own finding that the PR 'shipped an incomplete sweep,' slightly undercutting its usefulness.
